surface_or_claim: Recall routes are tenant-authenticated.
evidence: Recall, embed, status, sources, assistant, and memory routes are declared after the authenticated `/v1` boundary.
code_truth: Callers need a tenant context except public pre-auth routes elsewhere.
risk: This is a solid base for recall governance.
recommended_action: Keep routes authenticated and add explicit auth tests for recall endpoints.

surface_or_claim: Non-admin embed paths stay under the tenant recall root.
evidence: `/v1/embed` resolves every absolute path and checks it equals tenant root or starts with tenant root plus path separator.
code_truth: Regular tenants cannot ingest arbitrary server paths through this route.
risk: Good server-mounted path guard for self-hosted recall.
recommended_action: Extract a shared containment helper and test Windows and POSIX paths.

surface_or_claim: Recall namespaces are tenant-prefixed before qmd sees them.
evidence: `nsFor` sanitizes tenant and namespace and returns `kolm-<tenant>-<namespace>`.
code_truth: Two tenants using the same namespace do not collide in qmd collection names.
risk: This is a useful isolation primitive.
recommended_action: Keep tenant-prefixed namespaces and expose display names separately from internal names.

surface_or_claim: Recall status exposes qmd availability.
evidence: `/v1/recall/status` calls `recall.isAvailable()` and optional namespace status.
code_truth: Tenants can see whether the substrate is available.
risk: Useful for diagnostics, but not enough for compile correctness.
recommended_action: Keep status and add health semantics for unavailable versus empty.

surface_or_claim: Local RAG index/query is a real pure-JS path.
evidence: `node --check src/rag.js` and ESM import succeeded; code builds BM25 postings and query scores from local files.
code_truth: Local BM25 exists independently of qmd and network services.
risk: This is a solid local recall base.
recommended_action: Add fixture tests for index, query, list, and update behavior.

surface_or_claim: Artifacts can carry an optional `KOLMIDX` lookup container.
evidence: `encodeIndex` and `decodeIndex` use `KOLMIDX` magic and tolerate empty buffers.
code_truth: The artifact format has a safe placeholder for future index payloads.
risk: This supports an incremental path to artifact-bound recall.
recommended_action: Add fixtures for empty, valid, and corrupt index containers.

surface_or_claim: Verified wrap can use recall snippets when qmd returns chunks.
evidence: `POST /v1/wrap/verified` prepends retrieved snippets into the system prompt and returns `_kolm.recall_chunks`.
code_truth: This is the clearest shipped path where recall changes model input.
risk: Useful preview, with graceful degradation.
recommended_action: Expose whether grounding was unavailable versus empty.

surface_or_claim: Compile uses recalled chunks to shape the artifact.
evidence: `runJob` queries recall chunks and records `recall.done`.
code_truth: The retrieved chunks are not passed into `ctx.synthesize`, eval generation, or packaging except as a namespace marker.
risk: Artifacts can imply corpus grounding without corpus-derived behavior.
recommended_action: Feed chunks into synthesis/evals and store chunk hashes or label them unused.

surface_or_claim: Compiled artifacts include the populated recall index when a corpus is supplied.
evidence: `buildAndZip` receives `recall_namespace` but no `index` from the compile route.
code_truth: The manifest may show a recall namespace while `index.sqlite-vec` remains empty.
risk: Buyers may think corpus evidence is inside the file when it is external or absent.
recommended_action: Embed `KOLMIDX`/sqlite-vec payloads or mark artifacts as externally grounded.

surface_or_claim: `kolm compile --data` works for ordinary hosted SaaS local folders.
evidence: CLI sends `path.resolve(dataDir)` to hosted `/v1/embed`.
code_truth: The server route requires the path to exist on the server under the tenant recall root.
risk: Most remote users will silently compile without corpus after ingest failure.
recommended_action: Split local/offline data compile from server-mounted hosted embed and fail clearly.

surface_or_claim: `/v1/embed` returns the documented `{ indexed, skipped, namespace }` response.
evidence: `public/api.html` shows `indexed` and `skipped`.
code_truth: Actual route returns `namespace`, `tokenized`, `registered`, `embedded`, and `embed_error`.
risk: SDKs and docs can mis-handle ingest results.
recommended_action: Update API docs or normalize the route response.

surface_or_claim: Recall APIs return the caller's namespace.
evidence: `recall.ingest` returns internal `kolm-<tenant>-<namespace>` names, while `/v1/recall` returns the raw requested namespace.
code_truth: The same namespace can appear in two shapes across endpoints.
risk: Users and CLI output can confuse display namespace with qmd collection id.
recommended_action: Return both `namespace` and `internal_namespace` intentionally.

surface_or_claim: Hosted recall has an installed in-repo backend by default.
evidence: qmd is invoked as an external CLI or HTTP MCP service; `package.json` has no qmd dependency.
code_truth: Recall can be unavailable in default installs and queries degrade to empty.
risk: Default deployments may look like empty recall rather than unavailable recall.
recommended_action: Document qmd as required for hosted recall and surface unavailable state in queries.

surface_or_claim: Images, audio, video, and PDFs are fully tokenized locally.
evidence: Sidecar code supports text/code passthrough, optional pdf-parse, Anthropic image captions when keyed, and placeholders for audio/video/PDF fallback.
code_truth: CLIP, Whisper, CLAP, scene detection, and full local multimodal paths are not implemented here.
risk: Public multimodal claims can overstate shipped capability.
recommended_action: Publish a mode table and gate claims by dependency and configured backend.

surface_or_claim: Recall source preview uses the same strict tenant containment as embed.
evidence: `/v1/recall/sources/:id(*)` checks `full.startsWith(lookupRoot)`.
code_truth: The check is not separator-aware and is weaker than `/v1/embed`'s tenant-root check.
risk: Sibling-prefix paths can be mishandled on some path shapes.
recommended_action: Use a shared `isSubpath(root, child)` helper and test path traversal cases.

surface_or_claim: Recall source preview avoids leaking server paths or excessive text.
evidence: The route returns `sidecar` as an absolute path plus up to 4 KB preview after reading the whole file.
code_truth: Tenants can see server filesystem structure and preview sensitive sidecar text.
risk: Source debug output needs stricter data minimization.
recommended_action: Return relative ids, stream/cap reads, and audit source-preview access.

surface_or_claim: Attached local RAG indexes expose `lib.rag.query` to recipes.
evidence: `src/rag.js` defines `ragLibFor`, and CLI/help docs describe `lib.rag`.
code_truth: Source search found no `ragLibFor` integration in `runArtifact`; `compileJs` exposes `pack`, `index`, and `params`.
risk: Recipes that rely on `lib.rag` can fail despite successful attach.
recommended_action: Wire `ragLibFor` into `runArtifact` and add a fixture recipe test.

surface_or_claim: `docs/RAG.md` shipped status matches runtime behavior.
evidence: Docs say BM25 and `lib.rag` are shipped and exposed inside the recipe sandbox.
code_truth: The BM25 index/query path is real, but sandbox exposure is not proven or wired in current runner code.
risk: Docs can push users into an unsupported recipe API.
recommended_action: Downgrade `lib.rag` wording until runner integration and tests land.

surface_or_claim: `kolm rag attach` makes recall evidence part of the signed artifact.
evidence: Attach writes `<artifact>.rag.json` beside the artifact and docs note the artifact is not re-signed.
code_truth: The sidecar can change without invalidating the artifact signature.
risk: Signed-artifact claims should not include unsigned local sidecars.
recommended_action: Either embed and re-sign the index or label sidecar recall as local unsigned attachment.

surface_or_claim: Approved context is embedded inside every signed `.kolm`.
evidence: Recall meta copy says approved context is embedded inside every signed artifact.
code_truth: Current artifacts can have empty index slots and optional recall namespace metadata only.
risk: Hero-level copy conflicts with current artifact contents.
recommended_action: Use the honest disclosure wording as the primary claim until embedding ships.

surface_or_claim: Recall ships BM25 plus vector, RRF, and cross-encoder rerank.
evidence: Public Recall describes bge-m3, vector recall, RRF, and cross-encoder rerank.
code_truth: The repo delegates to qmd if available and local `kolm rag` is BM25 only.
risk: Specific model/retrieval claims need backend proof and benchmarks.
recommended_action: Scope vector/RRF/rerank to qmd-backed deployments and add smoke reports.

surface_or_claim: `/v1/recall` is multimodal for text, image, audio, video, and PDF.
evidence: API docs list multimodal recall support.
code_truth: Multimodal ingestion mostly creates placeholders unless optional/external tools are present, and recall query only reads qmd results.
risk: The endpoint can be marketed as broader than default behavior.
recommended_action: Document default modalities and dependency-gated modes.

surface_or_claim: Recall corpus is encrypted and durable by default.
evidence: Security page says recall corpus is in your namespace, encrypted, durable.
code_truth: Hosted sidecars are written next to source files; local BM25 indexes are JSON under `~/.kolm/rag`; no encryption or durability controls were found.
risk: Storage posture depends on deployment, not default code.
recommended_action: Tie encryption/durability claims to deployment controls and add local file permission policy.

surface_or_claim: Recall namespaces can be deleted or purged.
evidence: Source search found no recall purge/delete/retention route or CLI command.
code_truth: Corpus sidecars and local indexes can persist indefinitely.
risk: Regulated deployments need delete and retention controls.
recommended_action: Add namespace delete, sidecar cleanup, and local index removal workflows.

surface_or_claim: Recall/RAG behavior is launch-blocking tested.
evidence: Search found no tests for recall, embed, qmd, local RAG, `lib.rag`, or source preview boundaries.
code_truth: Recall claims can regress silently.
risk: Public recall and artifact-bound context claims can ship without executable proof.
recommended_action: Add route, unit, CLI, and artifact-runner tests before marketing recall as shipped.

surface_or_claim: `/v1/memory/recall` queries the memory/recall corpus.
evidence: The route searches the registry for concepts with an optional tag and runs matched concepts.
code_truth: It does not call `recall.query` or inspect qmd/local RAG indexes.
risk: The route name can mislead API users and docs authors.
recommended_action: Rename or document it as concept recall/composition, not corpus memory recall.

surface_or_claim: Assistant prompts never leave the user's box.
evidence: Assistant code is rule-based and does not call an LLM.
code_truth: It is still a hosted `/v1/assistant` route that receives prompts and can return tenant email/status data.
risk: The no-telemetry/no-box wording needs deployment context.
recommended_action: Phrase as no LLM round-trip and authenticated tenant-scoped server route.

surface_or_claim: Recall failures are visible to callers.
evidence: `recall.query` catches qmd failures and returns an empty array; wrap/compile also gracefully continue.
code_truth: Unavailable recall can be indistinguishable from no relevant chunks.
risk: Users may believe grounding was applied when it was not.
recommended_action: Return `available`, `degraded`, or `grounding_error` metadata in recall-dependent responses.

surface_or_claim: Local BM25 index avoids storing sensitive paths and previews.
evidence: `index.json` stores absolute paths in `docs[].abs`, manifest root, and document previews.
code_truth: Local indexes are easy to inspect and may expose source locations/content snippets.
risk: Users need explicit storage and redaction guidance.
recommended_action: Add opt-out for absolute paths/previews and document local index sensitivity.

surface_or_claim: Normative recall format matches v0.1 implementation.
evidence: Whitepaper marks `index.sqlite-vec` required if corpus supplied and shows recall keys such as embedder, chunks, and index hash.
code_truth: v0.1 implementation note says sqlite-vec is roadmap, while compile does not populate the index for corpus namespaces.
risk: Spec readers can overestimate conformance of current artifacts.
recommended_action: Separate normative future format from current implementation conformance tests.
